source_units.text_raw:
The Port Zone is intended to accommodate marine-related operations and supporting uses essential to the functioning of Charlottetown's harbour. This zone provides for shipping, ferry, cruise, fishing, and cargo-handling activities, along with associated industrial, commercial, and transportation facilities. The intent is to protect the Port's role as a regional economic driver and gateway, while ensuring safe and efficient marine operations, maintaining public access to selected waterfront areas, and managing land uses to minimize impacts on adjacent neighbourhoods and the downtown waterfront.
Rezoning from P to another zone is not permitted but the boundaries of the zoning map may change from time to time.
Uses Permitted by Site Plan Approval:
Events Hosting
Hostel / Hotel
Marina
Office
Retail Store
Transportation Service
Port Land Uses
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Marine Gas stations are permitted as-of- right.
Notwithstanding the accessory buildings which are permitted according to Section 3.1, port facilities operated by the Charlottetown Port Authority Inc. are permitted to include more than one building per lot, with no maximum or minimum zoning requirements.
A continuous linear boardwalk connection shall be provided along the waterfront, excluding wharfs and piers for the length of the Port Zone along the waterfront.
Any new building or development proposed in the Port Zone (P) is subject to the Regulations for Permitted Uses for the Downtown Waterfront (DW) Zone.
Two buildings per lot are permitted.
The maximum building height shall be 12 m or 3 storeys.
New buildings require a separation distance of 5m.
Port related facilities are exempt from any built for requirements such as:
Service and loading areas requirements or screening from public streets
Garage requirements
Any parking requirements
Setback and coverage requirements
Storage requirements
A land use buffer from adjacent abutting zones shall include:
No less than 5 m (16.4 ft) in width reserved for boardwalk, a linear trail, greenway, or landscaped area.
The land use buffer shall be planted with street trees and shrubs at a frequency of one tree for every 8 m (26.2 ft) of length along the abutting boundary line.
Direct Port related facilities are exempt from:
Any of the landscape requirements in Section 20.6.
Any safety fencing requirements which prohibit the use of razor wire or security fencing.
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
Lot Area min. 600 m 2 interior lot, 700m 2 corner lot
Frontage min. 20 m
Front Yard Setback min. 7 .5 m
Flanking Yard Setback min. 7 .5 m
Side Yard Setback min. 3 m, 6 m if bordering on any other
Rear Yard Setback min. 10 m, 20 m if bordering on any other zone
Lot Coverage max. 50%

review_flags.description: PDF text order and figure/table text were extracted with pypdf; verify section order, column flow, and page layout before normalization.
The zone contains table, figure, or schedule text. Values are preserved as extracted text and require source PDF layout review before normalization.
Some extracted text was not safely assigned to a labeled section and is preserved in content_blocks.
